---
title: "Class06 Homework"
author: "Qiman A1791240"
format:
  pdf:
    code-overflow: wrap
---

Q6. How would you generalize the original code above to work with any set of input protein structures?

Original Code:

```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
# Can you improve this analysis code?
library(bio3d)
s1 <- read.pdb("4AKE") # kinase with drug
s2 <- read.pdb("1AKE") # kinase no drug
s3 <- read.pdb("1E4Y") # kinase with drug
s1.chainA <- trim.pdb(s1, chain="A", elety="CA")
s2.chainA <- trim.pdb(s2, chain="A", elety="CA")
s3.chainA <- trim.pdb(s3, chain="A", elety="CA")
s1.b <- s1.chainA$atom$b
s2.b <- s2.chainA$atom$b
s3.b <- s3.chainA$atom$b
plotb3(s1.b, sse=s1.chainA, typ="l", ylab="Bfactor")
plotb3(s2.b, sse=s2.chainA, typ="l", ylab="Bfactor")
plotb3(s3.b, sse=s3.chainA, typ="l", ylab="Bfactor")
```

Generalized Code:

```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
library(bio3d)

#Input: pdb_ids: a character vector of PDB IDs. 
#chain: the protein chain to analyze, default is "A".

#What the function below does: reads each PDB structure,
#only keeps C-alpha atoms from the chosen chain, 
#extracts B-factors, plots the B-factor profile for each structure

#output: returns a list containing the trimmed PDB objects, 
#the extracted B-factor vectors, B-factor plots

analyze_pdb_bfactors <- function(pdb_ids, chain = "A") {

  # read and trim each structure
  trimmed_pdbs <- lapply(pdb_ids, function(id) {
    pdb <- read.pdb(id)
    trim.pdb(pdb, chain = chain, elety = "CA")
  })

  # extract B-factors
  b_factors <- lapply(trimmed_pdbs, function(pdb) {
    pdb$atom$b
  })
  
 # plot each structure
  for (i in seq_along(trimmed_pdbs)) {
    plotb3(
      b_factors[[i]],
      sse = trimmed_pdbs[[i]],
      typ = "l",
      ylab = "B-factor",
      main = paste("B-factor profile:", pdb_ids[i], "Chain", chain)
    )
  }

  # return results
  return(list(
    pdb_ids = pdb_ids,
    chain = chain,
    trimmed_pdbs = trimmed_pdbs,
    b_factors = b_factors
  ))
}

```

Example:

```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
result <- analyze_pdb_bfactors(c("4AKE", "1AKE", "1E4Y"), chain = "A")
```
